items.title: A circuit level investigation into the neuromodulatory effect of spinal cord stimulation for motor recovery
items.author: Daniel B. Rubin
items.content: <article>Spinal cord stimulation (SCS), an established treatment for chronic pain, may also restore motor function after spinal cord injury or stroke. In this issue of Neuron, Balaguer et al. dissect the circuit mechanism of SCS-enabled neuromodulation for motor recovery.</article>

items.title: Cortical interlaminar coherence: Recurrence is given, alignment is earned
items.author: Céline D. Dürst, Anthony Holtmaat
items.content: <article>Visual experience is essential for the proper development of sensory representations in layer (L) 2/3 of the visual cortex. In this issue of Neuron, Lempel et al. reveal that increased stimulus discriminability by feedforward inputs and their alignment to recurrent circuits are critical for the development of orientation selectivity.</article>

items.title: The ever-increasing heterogeneity of GABA neurons in the control of energy homeostasis
items.author: Tibor Harkany, Henning Fenselau
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Wang et al. expand the functional heterogeneity of hypothalamic neurocircuits by distinguishing cellular retinoic acid-binding protein 1-expressing neurons in the arcuate nucleus as powerful contributors to the regulation of energy expenditure.</article>

items.title: Metabolic stress, interneuron vulnerability, and cognitive decline
items.author: Afang Zhu, Yu Fu
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Landry et al. show how hippocampal cholecystokinin (CCK)-positive neurons respond to reduced glucose availability induced by a short-term high-fat diet and how changes in CCK neurons contribute to deficits in hippocampal-dependent cognitive function.</article>

items.title: What we can learn from the first personalized CRISPR-treated baby to tackle genetic brain disorders
items.author: Yang Yang
items.content: <article>The landmark report on personalized CRISPR genome editing to treat an infant (baby KJ) with a life-threatening liver disease sparked widespread attention, ushering in a new era of precision genetic intervention. This piece discusses the key challenges and opportunities in translating this milestone into treatments for genetic brain disorders.</article>
